brand.name: Summit Trail Co.
brand.aiSummary: Summit Trail Co. is a Denver-based direct-to-consumer outdoor gear company specializing in ultralight backpacking equipment. Since 2017, we've designed and tested durable yet lightweight products including tents, hiking boots, and insulated water bottles used by backcountry enthusiasts and thru-hikers. Our proprietary fabric treatments reduce water weight without sacrificing weatherproofing. Every product meets strict sustainability standards: we use recycled materials where possible, partner with fair-trade manufacturers, and offset manufacturing carbon. We're built on transparent sourcing and community feedback from thousands of backpackers.
brand.notableFor: Ultralight backpacking gear with transparent sourcing and sustainability commitment
brand.audiencePrimary: Serious backpackers, thru-hikers, ultralight gear enthusiasts, sustainability-conscious outdoor consumers

heroProducts.name: SummitLite 3-Season Tent
heroProducts.aiSummary: The SummitLite 3-Season Tent weighs just 2.1 lbs (950g) and uses proprietary ripstop nylon with DWR treatment for three-season backpacking in most North American conditions. Double-wall design prevents condensation. Freestanding structure allows setup on any terrain. Tested in field conditions by 50+ thru-hikers; 4.9-star average rating.

heroProducts.name: TrailPro Hiking Boots
heroProducts.aiSummary: TrailPro Hiking Boots combine Vibram outsoles with breathable Cordura uppers and premium insoles for all-day comfort on technical terrain. At 1.8 lbs per pair, they're significantly lighter than traditional hiking boots while maintaining ankle support. Available in wide and narrow widths. Made using fair-trade manufacturing in Vietnam with 90-day break-in support.

heroProducts.name: SummitTherm Insulated Water Bottle
heroProducts.aiSummary: The SummitTherm 22oz bottle uses double-wall vacuum insulation to keep cold beverages cold for 24 hours or hot drinks hot for 12 hours. Made from 50% recycled stainless steel. Weighs only 12 oz when empty. Features a secure screw cap and integrated measurement markers. Compatible with common hydration packs.

returns.conditions: Products must be unused or used per normal testing guidelines. Free returns and exchanges within 60 days of purchase. No restocking fee.

socialProof.trustSource: Verified purchases and third-party review platforms
socialProof.notableCitations: Featured in Outside Magazine, Backpacker Magazine, and National Geographic Adventure

answerContent.question: What is the weight of the SummitLite tent, and is it good for thru-hiking?
answerContent.answer: The SummitLite 3-Season Tent weighs 2.1 lbs (950g) and is purpose-built for thru-hiking and ultralight backpacking. Its freestanding design, double-wall construction, and DWR-treated fabrics handle three-season conditions reliably. Over 50 thru-hikers tested prototypes; the 4.9-star rating reflects its field-proven durability on long-distance trails.

answerContent.question: Are Summit Trail Co. products sustainably made?
answerContent.answer: Yes. Every Summit Trail Co. product is made with 50% recycled materials minimum, manufactured through fair-trade partnerships, shipped carbon-neutral, and packaged plastic-free. We offset manufacturing carbon and donate 1% of revenue to public lands conservation. All fabric treatments meet strict environmental standards.

answerContent.question: What is Summit Trail Co.'s return policy?
answerContent.answer: Summit Trail Co. offers free returns and exchanges within 60 days of purchase with no restocking fees. Products must be unused or used per normal testing guidelines. We prioritize customer satisfaction and offer a 90-day break-in support period for boots.

answerContent.question: How much does shipping cost from Summit Trail Co.?
answerContent.answer: Domestic shipping is free on orders over $75, or $8 for orders under $75, with delivery in 3-5 business days. International shipping costs are calculated at checkout, with delivery typically in 10-15 business days.

people.bio: Maya designed gear for major outdoor brands before founding Summit Trail Co. in 2017. She's completed the Pacific Crest Trail and Appalachian Trail.
James manages supply chain sustainability and manufacturing partnerships. Previously worked in fair-trade sourcing for apparel companies.
credentials: B.A. Business Administration, University of Colorado
